prompt: getMethod throws NoSuchMethodException. What is the bug?
choices: getMethod requires the method to be static
getMethod only finds public methods; secret() is private, so it throws NoSuchMethodException; use getDeclaredMethod instead, then setAccessible(true)
getMethod requires parameter types even for no-arg methods; pass an empty Class[] instead
instance methods cannot be found via getClass().getMethod()
explanation: Class.getMethod() searches public methods in the class and its superclasses. secret() is declared private, so it is invisible to getMethod(). Fix: use getDeclaredMethod("secret") to find it regardless of access level, then call m.setAccessible(true) before invoking.
